political.s: FEC: $133K in executive political donations; partisan lean split across 3 executive donors.

charity.s: $9M reported corporate giving in 2024 (~0.04% of revenue) via Sherwin-Williams Foundation. Operates the Sherwin-Williams Foundation: $2.5M in charitable contributions, gifts & grants paid (FY2023, IRS Form 990-PF).
charity.focus: Healthy housing, workforce development, community impact

environment.s: air pollution violation: $2.5M in federal penalties (Violation Tracker).

dei.s: EEO-1 workforce demographics on federal record (FY2020, DOL OFCCP FOIA release): 44,701 US employees — 24.8% women, 33.8% racial/ethnic minorities; management 25.6% women, 25.8% minorities.

wiki.industry: chemical industry, manufacture of paints, varnishes and similar coatings, printing ink a mastics
wiki.extract: Sherwin-Williams is an American paints and coatings company based in Cleveland, Ohio. It is primarily engaged in the manufacture, distribution, and sale of paints, coatings, floor coverings, and related products with operations in over 120 countries. It is the largest coatings company in the world by revenue.

cases.caseName: Terrazzo USA and Associates, Inc. v. National Terrazzo and Mosaic Association

cases.natureOfSuit: Civil Rights: Americans with Disabilities - Other

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy

health.s: 7 CPSC recalls on record; sample hazard: the aerosol canister can leak.

governance.s: Wikidata: owned by Dow Jones Industrial Average; HQ Cleveland. (Wikidata Q48881)

sampleRecords.event_description: Struck by running powered equipment  during maintenance, cleaning, testing
sampleRecords.final_narrative: A maintenance technician was troubleshooting the pail lid crimper on the production line in the filling department. The sensor activated and the crimper came down on the technician's hand, causing a fracture and laceration to his left middle fingertip.

sampleRecords.event_description: Injured by object handled by person  n.e.c.
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was delivering 5-gallon buckets of block filler and set a 60-pound bucket down on his little finger, crushing it. The employee sustained the amputation of the fingertip.

sampleRecords.event_description: Fall on same level due to slip or trip
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was delivering a paint order to a customer when he slipped on ice and fell backward, hitting his head on the ground. The employee suffered a contusion to the head.

sampleRecords.event_description: Other fall to lower level  unspecified
sampleRecords.final_narrative: On November 15, 2024, a driver was descending the ladder on his semi-truck and fell. The employee was hospitalized and required surgery.

sampleRecords.event_description: Fall on same level due to slip or trip
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was delivering paint to a customer location when they tripped and fell, resulting in a fractured hip.

tax.citation: Verified source: Institute on Taxation & Economic Policy (ITEP)

topHazards.label: The aerosol canister can leak, posing a fire hazard to consumers if the can is stored near a source of ignition.
The aerosol paint canister can leak, posing a fire hazard to consumers if the paint can is stored near a source of ignition.
The recalled scraper's body can break away from the handle when struck against a hard object. This poses a laceration hazard to users.
Overexposure to fumes, vapor or spray mist from the product can pose a serious respiratory hazard to consumers.
The aerosol cans can over-pressurize and explode, posing a risk of injury to consumers.

sampleRecalls.title: Sherwin-Williams Recalls Dupli-Color Automotive Paint Due to Fire Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves Dupli-Color Perfect Match™ automotive paint in green metallic. The liquid paint was sold in an 8-ounce spray paint can. Batch code "HL2031LN" is printed on the bottom of the can, along with UPC code 026916715036.
hazards: The aerosol paint canister can leak, posing a fire hazard to consumers if the paint can is stored near a source of ignition.

sampleRecalls.title: Sherwin-Williams Recalls Krylon Glaze and Acrylic Due to Fire Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves 12-ounce cans of Krylon ® Triple Thick Glaze and 11-ounce Krylon® Indoor/Outdoor Crystal Clear satin acrylic finish. Batch code HL1311FV, HL2081KL or HL2081MN is printed on the bottom of the can, along with UPC code 075577005000 or 724504035305. The Krylon logo is printed on the front of the white…
hazards: The aerosol canister can leak, posing a fire hazard to consumers if the can is stored near a source of ignition.
injuries: Sherwin-Williams has received 31 reports of leaking canisters, including reports of property damage from leaking paint. No injuries have been reported.
products: Krylon® Triple Thick Crystal Clear Glaze and Krylon® Indoor/Outdoor Crystal Clear Acrylic Paint

sampleRecalls.title: Sherwin-Williams Recalls Tree House Acrylic Coating Due to Fire Hazard; Sold Exclusively at Hobby Lobby
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves 11-ounce cans of Tree House Studio™ Clear Acrylic Matte Coating. The product was sold in a white aerosol can with Tree House Studio™ logo and the name "CLEAR ACRYLIC MATTE COATING" printed on the front. Batch date HL2081LT is printed on the bottom of the can, along with UPC code 724504103004.
hazards: The aerosol canister can leak, posing a fire hazard to consumers if the can is stored near a source of ignition.
products: Tree House Studio™ Clear Acrylic Matte Coating

sampleRecalls.title: Sherwin-Williams Recalls Purdy Scrapers Due to Laceration Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: The recalled scraper is a 2 ½" wide steel tool with a four-sided edge. The handle has a soft black grip with "Purdy" printed in red letters on the side of the handle.
hazards: The recalled scraper's body can break away from the handle when struck against a hard object. This poses a laceration hazard to users.
injuries: The firm has received four reports of the scraper's body separating from the handle. There have been no reported injuries.

sampleRecalls.title: Sherwin-Williams Co. Recalls Krylon UV Fabric Protector Due to Respiratory Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: The recall involves Krylon "Outdoor Spaces" UV Fabric Protector, which is an aerosol coating used to protect fabric. The part number (#2900) is printed above the UPC (724504029007) on the side of the can. The front of the 11-ounce aerosol can is tan with a picture of a patio containing outdoor furniture. "UV Fabric Pr…
hazards: Overexposure to fumes, vapor or spray mist from the product can pose a serious respiratory hazard to consumers.
injuries: Sherwin-Williams has received one report of an incident involving a consumer who experienced coughing and difficulty breathing requiring overnight hospitalization.
